summary:
Polycystic ovarian disease (PCOS) is characterized by persistently elevated luteinizing hormone (LH), an increased LH/FSH ratio, and raised serum dehydroepiandrosterone sulfate (DHEAS). Unlike these features, increased prolactin is not a typical or inherent part of PCOS; instead, it is associated with other endocrine disorders such as prolactinoma and does not represent a core pathological trait of PCOS.  

\boxed{\text{D}}

\boxed{\text{D}}. Answer choice: B
